UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents additional information about an authenticated user, complementing an external authentication system. Contains non-sensitive user profile data.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity. This typically matches the user's ID from an external authentication system.

firstName.description: The first name of the user, used for personalized greetings.

Trip.title: Trip
Trip.description: Represents a single trip plan, encompassing its core details, budget, and organizational information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Trip entity.

name.description: The name given to the trip by the organizer.

destination.description: The primary destination for the trip.

startDate.description: The planned start date of the trip.

endDate.description: The planned end date of the trip.

budgetPerHead.description: The estimated budget per person for the entire trip.

vibe.description: The overall style or nature of the trip (e.g., Budget, Mid-Range, Luxury).

organizerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who created and organizes this trip. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Trip)

status.description: The current planning status of the trip (e.g., Planning, Ongoing, Completed).

coverImageUrl.description: URL for a cover image representing the trip's destination.

totalPlannedBudget.description: The sum of planned budgets from all itinerary items for the trip.

totalActualBudget.description: The sum of actual expenses from all itinerary items for the trip.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trip record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the trip record was last updated.

TripMember.title: TripMember
TripMember.description: Represents an individual's participation in a specific trip, whether logged-in or as a guest.

id.description: Unique identifier for the TripMember entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this member belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N TripMember)

userId.description: Optional reference to the UserProfile if the member is logged in. (Relationship: UserProfile 0..1:N TripMember)

name.description: The name of the trip member, used for display and for guests without an account.

mobile.description: The mobile number of the trip member, primarily for guests joining without an account.

role.description: The role of the member within the trip (e.g., 'organizer', 'member').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trip member record was created.

ItineraryItem.title: ItineraryItem
ItineraryItem.description: Represents a single planned event, activity, or travel segment within a trip's daily schedule.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ItineraryItem entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this itinerary item belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N ItineraryItem)

dayNumber.description: The sequential day number of the trip on which this item is planned.

name.description: A descriptive name for the itinerary item (e.g., 'Dinner at Local Eatery', 'Flight to Goa').

category.description: The category of the itinerary item (e.g., 'stay', 'food', 'transport', 'activity', 'travel').

plannedBudget.description: The planned financial cost for this specific itinerary item.

actualBudget.description: The actual financial cost incurred for this specific itinerary item.

notes.description: Additional notes or details pertinent to this itinerary item.

mode.description: The mode of travel if the category is 'travel' (e.g., 'train', 'flight', 'roadtrip', 'bus').

fromLocation.description: The starting location for travel if the category is 'travel'.

toLocation.description: The destination location for travel if the category is 'travel'.

departureTime.description: The scheduled departure time for travel if the category is 'travel'.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the itinerary item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the itinerary item was last updated.

ChecklistItem.title: ChecklistItem
ChecklistItem.description: Represents a single item in a travel leg's preparation checklist, tracking its completion status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ChecklistItem entity.

itineraryItemId.description: Reference to the ItineraryItem (specifically a 'travel' category item) this checklist item belongs to. (Relationship: ItineraryItem 1:N ChecklistItem)

description.description: A description of the task or item to be checked off.

status.description: The current status of the checklist item ('red' for pending/critical, 'yellow' for in-progress, 'green' for complete).

order.description: A numeric value to define the display order of checklist items within a travel leg.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the checklist item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the checklist item was last updated.

Suggestion.title: Suggestion
Suggestion.description: Represents a trip idea or recommendation submitted by a trip member, potentially evaluated by AI.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Suggestion entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this suggestion belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N Suggestion)

link.description: A URL providing more details about the suggestion (e.g., hotel booking, activity page).

notes.description: User-provided additional context or thoughts about the suggestion.

addedByMemberId.description: Reference to the TripMember who submitted this suggestion. (Relationship: TripMember 1:N Suggestion)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the suggestion was added.

isAiRecommended.description: Indicates whether this suggestion has been recommended by the AI assistant.

aiReason.description: The AI-generated explanation or rationale for recommending this suggestion.

PackingItem.title: PackingItem
PackingItem.description: Represents a single item that needs to be packed for a trip, including who is responsible for it.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PackingItem entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this packing item belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N PackingItem)

name.description: The name of the item to be packed (e.g., 'Charger', 'Swimsuit').

assignedToMemberId.description: Optional reference to the TripMember assigned to pack this item. (Relationship: TripMember 0..1:N PackingItem)

isPacked.description: Boolean indicating whether the item has been packed or not.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the packing item was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the packing item was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profile data. This is private data, accessible only by the authenticated user whose `request.auth.uid` matches `{userId}`.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the authenticated user.

definition.description: The main collection for trip documents. In addition to the schema properties, each document includes a denormalized `members` map (e.g., `members: { [userId]: 'role' }`) to enable Authorization Independence and QAPs for listing trips. This map includes the `organizerId` and all `TripMember` `userId`s for quick authentication checks in security rules.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier for the trip.

definition.description: Details for each participant in a specific trip. While `role` is stored here, direct authorization for other collections relies on the denormalized `members` map in the parent `Trip` document (and its subcollections) to avoid `get()` calls in rules. `memberId` will typically be `userId` for logged-in members or a generated ID for anonymous members.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent trip.

params.name: memberId
params.description: The unique identifier for the trip member, often matching `userId` for logged-in users.

definition.description: Individual events, activities, or travel segments for a trip. Each document denormalizes the `organizerId` and the `members` map from its parent `Trip` document. This allows security rules to evaluate access directly on the document without needing to `get()` the parent `Trip` document.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent trip.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the itinerary item.

definition.description: Checklist items associated with a specific 'travel' category itinerary item. Each document denormalizes the `organizerId` and `members` map from its grandparent `Trip` document, ensuring authorization independence. Organizer has full write access; members have read-only access to status.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the grandparent trip.

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent itinerary item (a travel leg).

params.name: checklistItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the checklist item.

definition.description: Trip idea or recommendation submitted by a member. Each document denormalizes the `organizerId` and `members` map from its parent `Trip` document for authorization independence.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent trip.

params.name: suggestionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the suggestion.

definition.description: Items to be packed for a trip. Each document denormalizes the `organizerId` and `members` map from its parent `Trip` document for authorization independence.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent trip.

params.name: packingItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the packing item.

definition.description: A structurally segregated, read-only collection providing a public summary of a trip. This document contains a denormalized subset of `Trip` data and its `ItineraryItems` (potentially as an embedded array). It has a completely public read security posture to support the `/view/{tripId}` page, updated via server-side triggers from changes in `/trips/{tripId}`.

params.name: tripId
params.description: The unique identifier for the public trip summary.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed to strictly adhere to the core principles, especially Authorization Independence and QAPs, ensuring robust security rules and efficient data access. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** To completely avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies (i.e., `get()` calls in security rules), the crucial authorization context (`organizerId` and a `members` map) is denormalized directly into the `Trip` document and, subsequently, into every document within its subcollections (Itinerary Items, Checklist Items, Suggestions, Packing Items). This means that when a user attempts to access any document related to a trip (be it the trip itself or an item within its subcollections), the security rules can evaluate access by simply checking `request.auth.uid` against the `organizerId` or the `members` map present *within that specific document*, eliminating the need to read parent documents. This strategy not only simplifies rule logic but also prevents issues with atomic operations (transactions/batches) that arise from `get()` calls.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **For `/trips/{tripId}`:** The `Trip` collection is structured to support secure `list` operations. Each `Trip` document contains `organizerId` and a `members` map (e.g., `members: { [userId]: 'role' }`). A user can query for trips where their `request.auth.uid` matches the `organizerId` or is a key in the `members` map. This allows the client to fetch all relevant trips in a single query (e.g., `db.collection('trips').where('members.' + request.auth.uid, '!=', null)`), and the security rules can validate each document returned by verifying the `request.auth.uid` against the `resource.data.organizerId` or `resource.data.members` map on the read operation. This ensures that no unauthorized documents are returned, fulfilling the QAP.
2.  **For Subcollections (e.g., `/trips/{tripId}/itineraryItems/{itemId}`):** Because the `organizerId` and `members` map are denormalized into each subcollection document, `list` operations on these subcollections can also be secured without `get()` calls. For example, a query for `itineraryItems` within a `tripId` can be authorized by checking `request.auth.uid` against the `organizerId` or `members` map *within each `itineraryItem` document* during the list operation, ensuring only authorized items are accessible.
3.  **Structural Segregation for Public Data:** The `/public_trip_summaries/{tripId}` collection serves the public-facing view (`/view/{tripId}`). By creating a separate, denormalized collection with a simple `allow read: true` rule, public access is completely isolated from authenticated, collaborative trip data. This prevents accidental exposure of sensitive data and ensures optimal performance for public views without any authentication overhead.

**Other Principles:**
*   **DBAC:** User roles (`organizer`, `member`) are stored in the database (`organizerId` field and `members` map), eliminating custom claims. Authorization relies purely on `request.auth.uid` matching database entries.
*   **Clarity of Intent:** Path names like `/users/{userId}/profile` and `/trips/{tripId}/itineraryItems/{itemId}` are explicit. Denormalized fields are clearly named (`organizerId`, `members`).
*   **Access Modeling:** Consistent use of path-based ownership for user profiles, top-level collection for collaborative trips with denormalized membership, and hierarchical subcollections for nested trip data.
*   **Data Clarity:** `status` field for `Trip` state, `createdAt`/`updatedAt` timestamps, and `totalPlannedBudget`/`totalActualBudget` as denormalized aggregates ensure data predictability and integrity.

This design prioritizes secure and debuggable rules over strict data normalization, embracing denormalization as a key strategy for robust access control in Firestore.
